query: German potato pancakes Kartoffelpuffer served with French ratatouille

draft.title: Kartoffelpuffer with Ratatouille
draft.description: Crispy German potato pancakes served with a rich and savory French ratatouille vegetable stew.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.note: 1 tsp for Kartoffelpuffer, 1 tsp for ratatouille

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.note: freshly ground; half for Kartoffelpuffer, half for ratatouille

steps.text: Prepare the Kartoffelpuffer batter by combining grated potato, grated onion, egg, flour, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Mix well and let rest for 10 minutes.

Heat vegetable oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Spoon batter into the pan to form pancakes about 8 cm in diameter. Fry until golden and crisp, about 4 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add diced eggplant, zucchini, and bell peppers to the pan. Cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables soften, about 10 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes, dried thyme, oregano, salt, and pepper. Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in chopped fresh basil just before serving. Adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve the hot Kartoffelpuffer with a generous portion of ratatouille on the side.

warnings: Be careful when frying Kartoffelpuffer as the oil can splatter.
Ensure potatoes are well drained to avoid soggy pancakes.
